raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Approximately 20.3 million people ages 12 or older in the U.S. had a substance use disorder related to alcohol or illicit drugs in 2018.",
    "About 8% of Californians, or roughly 2.7 million people, met the criteria for substance use disorder in 2018.",
    "Only 10% of Californians with a substance abuse disorder received any type of assistance on the road to recovery.",
    "A substance use disorder can be prevented, treated, and managed to help people recover.",
    "As of November 2021, there were over 900 licensed residential treatment facilities in California, with a combined 20,464-bed capacity.",
    "The state of California spent more than $600 million on drug treatment for Medi-Cal recipients in the fiscal year 2020-21.",
    "During the fiscal year 2020-21, there were 61 complaints of deaths associated with substance abuse treatment facilities."
  ]
}
```
